channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Studying these young Alzheimer's patients led to breakthroughs. Trump cut the funding
item.description: Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.
item.content:encoded:
Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.

(Image credit: Juan Diego Reyes for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: As federal scientists faced turmoil, the Devils Hole pupfish reached a crisis point
item.description: The Devils hole pupfish lives in just one spot in Death Valley. Wildlife officials have managed this iconic fish for decades, and last spring, just as the Trump administration was laying off all kinds of scientists, the wild population of this fish plummeted to only 20 individuals. Officials then took an irrevocable step.
item.content:encoded:
The Devils hole pupfish lives in just one spot in Death Valley. Wildlife officials have managed this iconic fish for decades, and last spring, just as the Trump administration was laying off all kinds of scientists, the wild population of this fish plummeted to only 20 individuals. Officials then took an irrevocable step.

(Image credit: Olin Feuerbacher)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Dirty nickel: The cost of mining in Indonesia
item.description: Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.
item.content:encoded:
Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Making a podcast helped one family talk about aging, dementia and death
item.description: This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.
item.content:encoded:
This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.

(Image credit: Matthew Coughlin for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Researchers say closing a top USDA research lab will slow responses to honeybee deaths
item.description: When beekeepers saw honeybee die-offs last year, experts at a USDA research center stepped in to help. The Trump administration plans to close the facility, and beekeepers and scientists are worried.
item.content:encoded: When beekeepers saw honeybee die-offs last year, experts at a USDA research center stepped in to help. The Trump administration plans to close the facility, and beekeepers and scientists are worried.
item.dc:creator: Macy Byars

item.title: Preserving pollinators is good for health -- and income
item.description: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.
item.content:encoded: Pollinators have economic and health benefits, but those benefits have been difficult to quantify. A new study puts some numbers to how important pollinators are for both nutrition and income.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Why this tribe is buying up hundreds of acres of farmland — and flooding it
item.description: The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.
item.content:encoded:
The Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington state has been buying land in its traditional territory and removing levees. The goal is to turn farmland into wetlands with the hopes of restoring Chinook salmon.

(Image credit: Megan Farmer /KUOW)
item.dc:creator: John Ryan

item.title: Timmy the stranded whale rescued after weekslong effort
item.description: Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.
item.content:encoded:
Timmy captured the hearts of whale lovers across the globe who rooted for a happy ending for the humpback.

(Image credit: Sebastian Peters)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Why PEPFAR's top scientist stepped down
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talk with Mike Reid, the former chief science officer of PEPFAR, about why he resigned over concerns about America's global health strategy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talk with Mike Reid, the former chief science officer of PEPFAR, about why he resigned over concerns about America's global health strategy.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline
item.description: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.
item.content:encoded:
More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
